recipes.name: Spaghetti Carbonara
recipes.description: A rich, creamy Roman pasta made with eggs, Pecorino Romano, guanciale, and black pepper. No cream needed — just technique.
steps: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti until al dente.
While pasta cooks, fry guanciale in a cold pan over medium heat until the fat renders and it turns crispy. Remove pan from heat.
Whisk together eggs, extra yolks, and both grated cheeses in a bowl. Season very generously with black pepper.
Reserve at least 1 cup of pasta cooking water before draining.
Add hot drained pasta directly to the pan with guanciale (off the heat). Toss to coat in the fat.
Pour in the egg-cheese mixture and toss vigorously, adding pasta water a splash at a time until the sauce is silky and clings to the pasta.
Plate immediately and finish with more cheese and cracked black pepper.

recipes.name: Chicken Tikka Masala
recipes.description: Tender charred chicken swimming in a fragrant, creamy tomato sauce. Marinating overnight makes all the difference.
ingredients: Basmati rice and naan to serve
steps: Mix chicken with yogurt and tikka masala paste. Cover and marinate in the fridge for at least 1 hour, preferably overnight.
Grill or broil the marinated chicken on high heat until charred in spots and cooked through, about 8 minutes. Set aside.
Melt butter in a large heavy pan over medium heat. Add onion and cook until deep golden, about 10 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger, stir for 2 minutes. Add cumin and turmeric, cook 30 seconds.
Pour in tomatoes and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes until the sauce thickens and darkens.
Optional: blend the sauce until smooth, then return to pan.
Add the grilled chicken and cream. Simmer gently for 10 minutes. Stir in garam masala and adjust seasoning.
Garnish with cilantro and serve with rice and warm naan.

recipes.name: Avocado Toast
recipes.description: The humble classic done right — perfectly ripe avocado, seasoned well, on properly toasted sourdough. Endlessly customizable.
steps: Toast the bread until deeply golden and crunchy — don't under-toast it.
Halve and pit the avocados. Scoop flesh into a bowl.
Add lemon juice and a generous pinch of flaky salt. Mash with a fork to your preferred texture — I like it still a bit chunky.
Pile the avocado onto the toast and spread to the edges.
Drizzle with olive oil. Top with red pepper flakes, more flaky salt, and any garnishes you like.
Add a poached or fried egg on top if you want to make it a full meal.

recipes.name: Beef Tacos al Pastor
recipes.description: Street-style tacos with marinated beef, charred pineapple, and all the fixings. Quick to assemble once the marinade is done.
steps: Mix achiote paste, vinegar, cumin, oregano, and garlic into a marinade. Coat beef slices well and leave for at least 15 minutes (longer is better).
Heat a cast-iron pan or griddle until very hot. Cook pineapple slices until charred on both sides. Chop roughly and set aside.
In the same pan, cook beef in batches on high heat for 2–3 minutes until caramelized at the edges.
Warm tortillas directly on a gas flame or in a dry pan until blistered.
Build tacos: beef, charred pineapple, onion, cilantro. Finish with salsa verde and a squeeze of lime.

recipes.name: Tom Yum Soup
recipes.description: Thailand's most iconic soup — hot, sour, and deeply aromatic. The lemongrass and kaffir lime leaves are non-negotiable.
ingredients: 1 litre good chicken or seafood stock
2 stalks lemongrass, bruised and cut into 3cm pieces
1 tsp palm sugar or brown sugar
1 can (400ml) coconut milk (for tom kha variation)
steps: Bring stock to a boil in a large pot. Add lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, and chillies.
Simmer the aromatics for 10 minutes to infuse the broth.
Add mushrooms and cook 3 minutes.
Add prawns and cook just until pink and curled, about 2 minutes.
Season with fish sauce, lime juice, and sugar. Taste and adjust — it should be aggressively sour, salty, and spicy.
Serve immediately in bowls, garnished generously with fresh cilantro.

recipes.name: Fluffy Banana Pancakes
recipes.description: Naturally sweetened banana pancakes that are soft, fluffy, and require no electric mixer. A perfect lazy weekend breakfast.
ingredients: Maple syrup and fresh berries to serve
steps: Mash bananas thoroughly in a large bowl — the riper, the better.
Whisk in eggs and milk until combined.
Sift in flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt. Fold gently until just combined — a few lumps are fine.
Melt a knob of butter in a non-stick pan over medium-low heat.
Pour about ¼ cup of batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set, about 2–3 minutes.
Flip carefully and cook 1–2 minutes more. Repeat with remaining batter.
Serve with maple syrup and fresh berries.

recipes.name: Classic Caesar Salad
recipes.description: A proper Caesar with a punchy homemade dressing, house-made croutons, and a shower of real Parmesan. Worth every anchovy.
ingredients: 2 large heads romaine lettuce, roughly torn
4 anchovy fillets, minced to a paste
For croutons: 3 slices day-old sourdough, 2 tbsp olive oil, garlic powder
steps: Make croutons: tear bread into chunks, toss with olive oil and garlic powder, toast in a 200°C oven for 10 minutes until golden.
Make dressing: whisk together anchovies, garlic, mustard, Worcestershire, and lemon juice. Whisk in egg yolk.
Slowly drizzle in olive oil while whisking constantly until emulsified. Stir in half the Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.
In a large bowl, toss romaine with just enough dressing to coat each leaf.
Top with croutons and remaining Parmesan. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Slow-Cooked Pulled Pork
recipes.description: Low and slow is the only way. Smoky, fall-apart pork shoulder that rewards patience. Makes incredible sandwiches all week long.
ingredients: Brioche buns and coleslaw to serve
steps: Combine all the dry spices into a rub. Score the pork shoulder all over and massage the rub in generously.
Ideally, wrap and refrigerate overnight. Otherwise, rest at room temperature for 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 150°C (300°F). Place pork in a roasting tin, add vinegar to the bottom. Cover tightly with foil.
Roast for 7–8 hours until the meat is completely tender and pulls apart with a fork.
Remove foil, increase oven to 220°C (425°F), and roast uncovered for 20 more minutes to crisp the outside.
Rest for 20 minutes, then pull apart with two forks into big shreds.
Serve piled high on brioche buns with coleslaw and BBQ sauce.

recipes.name: Vegan Buddha Bowl
recipes.description: A vibrant, nourishing bowl packed with roasted vegetables, grains, and a tahini-lemon dressing that ties it all together.
ingredients: For dressing: 3 tbsp tahini, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 garlic clove, 2–3 tbsp water, salt
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C. Toss sweet potato and chickpeas with olive oil, salt, and your choice of spices. Spread on a baking sheet.
Roast for 25 minutes, adding broccoli halfway through, until everything is roasted and the chickpeas are crispy.
Make dressing: blend or whisk together tahini, lemon juice, minced garlic, and enough water to make it pourable. Season with salt.
Warm the quinoa or rice and divide between bowls.
Arrange spinach, roasted vegetables, chickpeas, and avocado on top.
Drizzle generously with tahini dressing and serve.

recipes.name: Chocolate Lava Cake
recipes.description: Individual chocolate cakes with a molten, flowing centre. Looks impossibly impressive, but the batter can be made hours ahead.
ingredients: 150g unsalted butter, plus extra for ramekins
steps: Butter four ramekins and dust with cocoa powder, tapping out any excess. Place on a baking tray.
Melt chocolate and butter together in a heatproof bowl over simmering water (or in short microwave bursts). Stir until smooth. Cool slightly.
Whisk eggs, egg yolks, and sugar together until the mixture is pale and thick, about 3 minutes.
Fold the chocolate mixture into the egg mixture until combined. Sift in flour and salt, fold gently.
Divide batter among ramekins. Refrigerate for at least 20 minutes (or up to 24 hours).
Preheat oven to 200°C. Bake for 10–12 minutes — the edges should be set but the center should wobble when shaken.
Run a knife around the edge, invert onto plates, and serve immediately with vanilla ice cream.

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Shrimp
recipes.description: Plump prawns seared in a shamelessly buttery, garlicky sauce. On the table in 20 minutes and tastes like a restaurant dish.
ingredients: 100ml dry white wine or chicken stock
Large handful of fresh parsley, chopped
Crusty bread or pasta to serve
steps: Pat prawns dry with paper towel and season well with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over high heat until shimmering. Add prawns in a single layer.
Sear without moving for 1.5 minutes until pink and caramelized on one side. Flip and cook 30 more seconds. Remove from pan.
Reduce heat to medium. Add butter and garlic to the same pan and cook until garlic is fragrant and just starting to turn golden, about 1 minute.
Add red pepper flakes and white wine. Let it bubble and reduce by half, scraping any bits from the pan.
Return prawns to the pan. Add lemon juice and parsley. Toss to coat.
Serve immediately with crusty bread to mop up the sauce.

recipes.name: Tonkotsu Ramen
recipes.description: A deeply porky, milky-white broth that takes time but is absolutely worth it. Topped with chashu, soft-boiled eggs, and noodles.
ingredients: 4 soft-boiled eggs, marinated in soy and mirin
Sesame oil, nori, bamboo shoots to garnish
steps: Blanch trotters and neck bones in boiling water for 5 minutes. Drain and rinse thoroughly under cold water.

Place blanched bones in a large pot with enough cold water to cover by 5cm. Bring to a vigorous boil and keep at a rolling boil for 3–4 hours, adding water as needed, until the broth turns white and creamy.
While broth cooks, make chashu: roll and tie pork belly, sear all over, braise in soy, mirin, sake, and sugar for 2 hours.
Char onion halves directly on a gas flame or under the broiler. Add to broth with garlic for the last hour.
Strain broth. Season with soy sauce, mirin, and salt to taste.
Marinate peeled soft-boiled eggs in a 1:1 mix of soy sauce and mirin for at least 2 hours.
Cook noodles per package. Divide into bowls, ladle over hot broth, and top with chashu slices, halved eggs, spring onions, nori, and a drizzle of sesame oil.

recipes.name: Margherita Pizza
recipes.description: Neapolitan-style pizza at home. A hot oven, quality ingredients, and restraint — less is more with a great Margherita.
ingredients: For dough: 300g 00 flour, 200ml warm water, 7g instant yeast, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp olive oil
200g San Marzano tomatoes, crushed by hand
steps: Make dough: combine flour, salt, and yeast. Add warm water and olive oil. Knead 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover and rise 1 hour.
Preheat your oven to its maximum temperature (ideally 280°C+) with a pizza stone or steel inside.
Season crushed tomatoes lightly with salt. Do not cook them.
Divide dough in two. Stretch each piece on a lightly floured surface into a thin round — use your hands, not a rolling pin.
Spread a thin layer of tomatoes, leaving a 2cm border. Scatter over the mozzarella.
Slide onto the hot stone and bake for 8–10 minutes until the crust is charred at the edges and the cheese is bubbling.
Top with fresh basil and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil immediately after baking.

recipes.name: Greek Salad
recipes.description: A no-cook salad built on ripe tomatoes, cucumber, olives, and a generous slab of feta. Olive oil is the dressing — that's all you need.
ingredients: 4 large ripe tomatoes, cut into wedges
1 large cucumber, cut into thick half-moons
½ small red onion, thinly sliced
steps: Arrange tomatoes, cucumber, pepper, and onion in a large bowl or on a platter. Don't toss — keep it rustic.
Scatter over the olives.
Place the whole feta block on top and crumble it slightly — you want big chunks, not crumbles.
Drizzle generously with olive oil.
Sprinkle dried oregano over everything and season with salt and black pepper.
Serve immediately with crusty bread.

recipes.name: French Onion Soup
recipes.description: Caramelized onions, rich beef broth, and a bubbling Gruyère crust. One of the great soups of the world, worth every hour of stirring.
ingredients: For the crouton: 4 thick slices sourdough
steps: Melt butter with olive oil in a large heavy pot over medium-low heat. Add all the sliced onions and stir well to coat.
Cook onions, stirring every 5–10 minutes, for 60–70 minutes. They will wilt, then slowly turn golden, then deep amber. Patience is everything here. Add sugar halfway through to help caramelization.
Deglaze with white wine, scraping up any sticky bits from the bottom. Let it bubble for 2 minutes.
Add beef stock, thyme, and bay leaves. Simmer for 20 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Remove thyme and bay.
Toast sourdough slices until golden.
Ladle hot soup into oven-safe bowls. Place a bread slice on top, pile over the Gruyère generously.
Broil under the grill until the cheese is melted, bubbling, and dark in spots. Serve immediately — carefully, the bowls will be very hot.
